narrative: Annaly is a ~190-person mortgage REIT that nonetheless custodies consumer borrower PII at scale (Onslow Bay whole loans + MSR servicing ~608k loans). DLP value = catch the borrower PII (loan tapes), MNPI (positions/hedging/earnings), and counterparty wire instructions — while staying quiet on the CUSIPs/pool numbers/trade IDs their trading work is saturated with. Open on a loan tape, show the CUSIP precision inversion, then MNPI and wires.

findings.finding: Internally-managed mortgage REIT (NYSE: NLY) across four segments: Agency MBS, Residential Credit (Onslow Bay), MSR, and Corporate; spread investing funded by repo with heavy hedging.

MSR platform services ~608,000 loans / $192B UPB (Rocket as subservicer); Onslow Bay buys residential whole loans — so Annaly holds loan-level borrower NPI (SSN, address, DOB, loan number) despite being a markets firm.
~190 employees manage a large balance sheet — high value-per-person, with a small security/ops surface relative to the sensitivity of the borrower data and MNPI it touches.
GLBA (borrower NPI), SEC/Reg FD + insider trading (public company → MNPI), SOX, state privacy. Residential credit & servicing add consumer-finance obligations.
As an agency-MBS trader, Annaly's files are saturated with CUSIPs, pool numbers, and trade IDs — public identifiers that look sensitive; a CUSIP detector firing on them is pure alert-fatigue.
findings.source: inference from Agency MBS trading business

findings.finding: In the playground scan, strong-pattern types (SSN, IBAN, CUSIP) fire even in CSV, but borrower NAME / ADDRESS / driver's-license and some fields are missed when packed in dense loan-tape / wire CSV rows (they fire fine in prose). For a firm whose crown-jewel data is bulk loan tapes, this tabular under-detection is the recall story.
findings.source: observed: scan.py over the generated carriers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Borrower loan-level PII at scale — whole-loan tapes (Onslow Bay) and MSR servicing files (~608k loans) carry bulk borrower SSN/name/DOB/address/loan#. The crown-jewel GLBA leak for a markets firm not built around consumer-data security.
hypotheses.basis: MSR services ~608k loans; Onslow Bay acquires whole loans.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CUSIP / securities-identifier precision inversion — CUSIPs, pool numbers, trade IDs are public identifiers their work is full of; a detector flagging them is unusable noise. The value is staying quiet on securities data while catching real PII.
hypotheses.basis: Agency-MBS trading produces CUSIPs/pool numbers constantly; a CUSIP detector exists.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MNPI / confidential markets info — portfolio positions, hedging, securitization pipeline, pre-release earnings, board decks — Reg FD + insider-trading exposure. Content detection, not regex PII.
hypotheses.basis: Public REIT; positions/hedging are material non-public information.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Counterparty wire / settlement instructions — repo financing and large-dollar wires expose bank accounts, ABA routing, SWIFT/IBAN in treasury emails/spreadsheets.
hypotheses.basis: Repo is Annaly's core funding; settlement requires counterparty wire details.

decisions.choice: borrower PII at scale + the CUSIP precision inversion
decisions.rationale: The two most distinctive, demo-resonant points: a markets firm holding 608k borrowers' NPI, and the need to not drown them in CUSIP alerts.
decisions.drove: loan tape + MSR carriers as the lead PII; a positions/trade carrier full of CUSIPs as the precision near-miss
7 carriers across Email/Drive/Snowflake/GitHub/Slack, ~2 dozen indexed plants

decisions.choice: Email, Google Drive, Snowflake, GitHub, Slack
decisions.rationale: Where loan tapes, wires, MNPI, servicing data, and analytics secrets actually live.

decisions.choice: Portfolio/Agency, Onslow Bay/Residential Credit, MSR/Servicing, Treasury/Repo, Finance/SEC, Risk Analytics Eng, HR
decisions.rationale: Defining personas are the trading desk (MNPI/CUSIPs) and the loan-ops teams (borrower PII), not a generic support org.
decisions.drove: loan tape→Onslow Bay; MNPI→portfolio/finance; wires→treasury; secrets→analytics eng

decisions.choice: CUSIPs, pool numbers, trade IDs, loan numbers shaped like SSNs, placeholder keys
decisions.rationale: The work-product inversion: their everyday securities identifiers look sensitive but are public; over-firing is the alert-fatigue story.
decisions.drove: the positions/trade carrier tagged fp_near_miss; cusip mapped to AMERICAN_BANKERS_CUSIP_ID so the report card shows it firing

decisions.choice: spaced borrower SSN in a tape/Slack, wire account spelled out, MNPI in prose
decisions.rationale: Recall on whitespace-fragile and content-only cases.
decisions.drove: tagged fn_evasive plants in MSR/Slack/treasury/MNPI carriers

decisions.choice: PII (borrower), financial (wires), secret (analytics keys), confidential (MNPI); no PCI
decisions.rationale: Annaly doesn't process payment cards; cards would be irrelevant padding.

open_questions: Exact analytics/risk stack assumed (Bloomberg/market-data vendor, warehouse) — would refine which secret types to plant.
CUSIPs are synthesized to shape (9-char) — real CUSIP check-digit validity not guaranteed; fine for the alert-noise demo.
Document carriers (.xlsx loan tape, .pdf board deck) not included — text/CSV/JSON/eml only.
Recall is ~50% mostly because borrower PII in dense CSV/tabular form under-detects vs prose — a genuine, prospect-relevant gap (loan tapes), not a generator bug; values fire in clean context.
